info.title: HiveMQ Cloud Public REST API
info.description:
# Introduction

HiveMQ Cloud offers REST APIs designed to seamlessly extend your MQTT workflows programmatically.
These APIs enable users to perform a variety of actions related to client credential management and access control.

HiveMQ Cloud REST API provides endpoints for the following use cases:
- Listing all MQTT Credentials
- Listing all roles and permissions
- Assign and un-assign permissions to roles
- Assign and un-assign user to roles
- Listing all MQTT Clients
- Getting detailed information about a specific MQTT client
- Listing all subscriptions for a specific MQTT client
- Getting the connection status for a specific MQTT client

get.description:
Searches clients based on query parameters. Filters are grouped by type: boolean, string, and number.

Available columns:
- ID (string, primary key): The MQTT client identifier.
- CONNECTED (boolean): Whether the client is currently connected.
- CONNECTED_NODE (string): The node the client is connected to.
- IP_ADDRESS (string): The IP address of the client.
- USER_NAME (string): The username the client uses.
- MAX_QUEUE_SIZE (number): The maximum queue size for the client.
- CURRENT_QUEUE_SIZE (number): The current queue size for the client.
- TIMESTAMP (number): The timestamp of the last session update.

Filter types:
- Boolean filters: `type:value` (for example, CONNECTED:true)
- String filters: `type:operation:value` (for example, ID:EQ:client1, USER_NAME:NOT_EQ:admin)
- Number filters: `type:operation:value` (for example, MAX_QUEUE_SIZE:GT:100, TIMESTAMP:LTE:1700000000)

Supported operations for string filters: EQ (equal to), NOT_EQ (not equal to), CONTAINS (contains), STARTS_WITH (starts with), ENDS_WITH (ends with), REGEX (regular expression).
Supported operations for number filters: EQ (equal to), NOT_EQ (not equal to), GT (greater than), GTE (greater than or equal to), LT (less than), LTE (less than or equal to).


Examples:
- Single boolean filter: CONNECTED:true
- Single string filter: ID:EQ:client1
- Single number filter: MAX_QUEUE_SIZE:GT:100
- Multiple filters: ID:EQ:client1, CONNECTED:true, IP_ADDRESS:NOT_EQ:127.0.0.1, MAX_QUEUE_SIZE:LTE:500

Example search query URLs:
- /api/v2/orgs/{orgId}/clusters/{clusterId}/a/mqtt/clients/search?boolean-filter=CONNECTED:true
- /api/v2/orgs/{orgId}/clusters/{clusterId}/a/mqtt/clients/search?string-filter=ID:EQ:client1
- /api/v2/orgs/{orgId}/clusters/{clusterId}/a/mqtt/clients/search?number-filter=MAX_QUEUE_SIZE:GT:100
- /api/v2/orgs/{orgId}/clusters/{clusterId}/a/mqtt/clients/search?string-filter=ID:EQ:client1&boolean-filter=CONNECTED:true&string-filter=IP_ADDRESS:NOT_EQ:127.0.0.1&number-filter=MAX_QUEUE_SIZE:LTE:500

This endpoint requires at least HiveMQ version 4.42.0. on all cluster nodes.

parameters.description: List of boolean search filters for client search query.
parameters.name: boolean-filter

parameters.description: List of string search filters for client search query.

parameters.description: List of number search filters for client search query.

get.summary: Search and filter clients via GET parameters

get.description: This API allows you to access various performance and operational metrics for the MQTT broker. These metrics help you monitor the health and activity of your HiveMQ Cloud broker, providing insights into client connections, message delivery, and overall system performance.

200.description: The response contains a variety of metrics categorized into key areas such as client connections, message delivery, and subscriptions. Each metric offers insights into specific aspects of the broker's operation to help you effectively monitor, manage, and optimize your IoT infrastructure.

text/plain.example:
The following example shows the type of metric data the API returns. Each metric includes a brief description, the metric type, and the current value.
- Client Connections Metrics:
  hivemq_cloud_keep_alive_disconnect_count:
    Description: The number of connections closed because the client missed sending a keep-alive message during the keep-alive interval.
    Type: Gauge
    Value: 0
- Message Delivery Metrics:
  hivemq_cloud_messages_incoming_connect_count:
    Description: The total number of incoming MQTT CONNECT messages received.
    Type: Gauge
    Value: 48,963
  hivemq_cloud_messages_dropped_queue_full_count:
    Description: The number of messages dropped because the queue of a disconnected client with a persistent session was full.
    Type: Gauge
    Value: 0
- Subscription Metrics:
  hivemq_cloud_subscriptions_overall_current:
    Description: The current number of subscriptions on the broker.
    Type: Gauge
    Value: 0

get.description:
Lists all MQTT credentials for the specified cluster.

This endpoint uses cursor-based pagination.
The results are not sorted in any way, no ordering of any kind is guaranteed.

parameters.description: Specifies the page size for the returned results. The value must be between 50 and 2500. The default page size is 500.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The cursor that has been returned by the previous result page. Do not pass this parameter if you want to fetch the first page.
parameters.name: cursor

get.summary: List all permissions for a specific role by role ID or role name
get.description:
Retrieves all permissions assigned to a role. The `roleIdOrName` parameter accepts either:
- **Numeric value** (e.g., `123`): Interpreted as a role ID. The system looks up the role by its numeric identifier.
- **Text value** (e.g., `admin`, `read-only`): Interpreted as a role name. The system looks up the role by its unique name.

**Lookup behavior:**
- If a numeric ID is provided and the role exists, permissions are returned immediately.
- If a numeric ID is provided but the role is not found, the system attempts to find a role with that exact value as its name (edge case for numeric role names).
- If a text name is provided, the system looks up the role by name.
- Returns 404 if the role is not found by either ID or name.

parameters.name: roleIdOrName
parameters.description: Role ID (numeric) or role name (text). Examples - numeric ID `123`, text name `admin` or `read-only`

200.description: Successfully retrieved permissions for the specified role

404.description: Cluster not found. The specified cluster does not exist, or the current user does not have permission to access it.

200.description: Attached permission to role for the given cluster.

404.description: Cluster not found. The specified cluster does not exist, or the current user does not have permission to access it.

get.description:
Lists all client sessions (online and offline) known to the whole HiveMQ cluster.

The result contains each client's client identifier. For more details about each client you can call the endpoints that have a clientId in their URL.

This endpoint uses pagination with a cursor.
The results are not sorted in any way, no ordering of any kind is guaranteed.

This endpoint requires at least HiveMQ version 4.4.0. on all cluster nodes.

parameters.description: Specifies the page size for the returned results. The value must be between 50 and 2500. The default page size is 500.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The cursor that has been returned by the previous result page. Do not pass this parameter if you want to fetch the first page.
parameters.name: cursor

empty-results.description: Empty result if no clients are known.
empty-results.summary: Empty result if no clients are known

multiple-results-last-page.description: Example response with multiple clients. No more pages left.

multiple-results-pages-left.description: Example response with multiple clients. More pages left.

single-result.description: Example response with one known client.

delete.description:
Invalidates the client session for a client with the given client identifier. If the client is currently connected, it will be disconnected as well.

 If your client identifiers contain special characters, please make sure that the clientId is URL encoded (a.k.a. percent-encoding, as in RFC 3986).

parameters.description: The MQTT client identifier.
parameters.name: clientId

parameters.description: Whether to prevent the will message.
parameters.name: preventWillMessage

param-invalid.summary: Query parameter can not be parsed

get.description:
Returns detailed information for a specific client with it is current state.

 Including all session and connection information.
If your client identifiers contain special characters, please make sure that the clientId is URL Encoded (a.k.a. percent-encoding, as in RFC 3986).

parameters.description: The MQTT client identifier.
parameters.name: clientId

online-client-tls-proxy.description: Online client with TLS and Proxy Protocol enabled

404.description: Resource not found

delete.description:
Disconnects a specific client if its is currently connected.

 If your client identifiers contain special characters, please make sure that the clientId is URL Encoded (a.k.a. percent-encoding, as in RFC 3986).

parameters.description: The MQTT client identifier.
parameters.name: clientId

parameters.description: Whether to prevent the will message.
parameters.name: preventWillMessage

get.description:
Returns the information if a specific client is currently connected.

 If you are only interested in the connection status of a client prefer this endpoint over the the full client detail.
If your client identifiers contain special characters, please make sure that the clientId is URL Encoded (a.k.a. percent-encoding, as in RFC 3986).

parameters.description: The MQTT client identifier.
parameters.name: clientId

404.description: Resource not found

get.description:
List all subscriptions for a specific client.

This endpoint does not support pagination with cursor at the moment, but it might be added in future versions. Please make sure to check if a cursor is returned and another page is available to have a future-proof implementation.

parameters.description: The MQTT client identifier.
parameters.name: clientId
